title: Scenario
description: An individual scenario definition file. Describes one conversation practice session.

id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier within the pack.

title.description: Display title shown in the scenario list and header.

description.description: Short description shown in the library before the player starts.

player_role.description: Explains who the player is in this scenario (shown in the setup screen).

difficulty.description: How challenging the NPC will be.

duration_minutes.description: Suggested play time in minutes shown to the player.

npc_ref.description: ID of the NPC file to use for this scenario (must match an npc.id in the pack).

rubric_ref.description: ID of the rubric to use for evaluation. Optional — omit to disable structured scoring.

opening_context.description: Scene-setting text shown to the player before the first NPC turn.

state_defaults.description: Starting values (0–100) for all conversation state variables.

trust.description: How much the NPC trusts the player at the start.

patience.description: How patient the NPC is at the start.

pressure.description: How much situational pressure the NPC feels at the start.

rapport.description: Level of positive connection between NPC and player at the start.

openness.description: How open the NPC is to the player's ideas at the start.

objective_progress.description: How much of the player's goal has been achieved. Usually starts at 0.

endings.description: Possible outcomes when the session ends.

label.description: Human-readable label shown on the debrief screen.

condition.description: Simple expression evaluated against state variables at session end (e.g. 'objective_progress >= 75').

npc_reaction.description: What the NPC says when this ending is triggered.

examples.title: Behavioral Interview
examples.description: Practice STAR-method behavioral questions with a fictional hiring manager.
examples.player_role: You are interviewing for a software engineering position at a mid-sized tech company.
goals: Answer with the STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result)
Ask at least one thoughtful question
examples.opening_context: You've arrived for your interview. Jordan greets you and gestures to a chair across the desk.

endings.label: Offer Extended
endings.npc_reaction: I was genuinely impressed. We'd like to move you to the next round.

endings.label: No Offer
endings.npc_reaction: Thanks for coming in. We'll be in touch if there's a fit.
